description:
You are acting as a release-manager who must prepare a clean list of application versions before pushing them to the deployment system.

1. A text file already exists:  
   /home/user/releases/versions.txt  

2. Exact present contents of /home/user/releases/versions.txt (8 lines, shown with the required final line-feed on the last line):

1.0.0  
2.0.0  
1.5.0  
2.1.0  
1.0.0  
3.0.0  
2.1.0  
1.2.0  

3. Goal – produce a *deduplicated* and *descending-sorted* list of these semantic version numbers and save it to a **new file** called  
   /home/user/releases/latest_versions.txt  

4. Strict output requirements for /home/user/releases/latest_versions.txt:  
   • Plain UTF-8 text.  
   • Exactly six lines.  
   • Each line contains **only** the version string, no trailing or leading spaces.  
   • A single UNIX newline (LF) character terminates every line, including the last one.  
   • The lines, in order from top to bottom, must be:

3.0.0  
2.1.0  
2.0.0  
1.5.0  
1.2.0  
1.0.0  

5. Use ANY single, valid shell command (piped or otherwise) to create the file. No manual editing; it must be generated from versions.txt.

6. When finished, /home/user/releases/latest_versions.txt must exist with the exact contents and formatting described above so automated verification can simply read and compare the file.
truth:
Initial state (before agent starts):
• Directory /home/user/releases already exists with permissions 755.
• /home/user/releases/versions.txt exists with permissions 644 and content (including final LF):

1.0.0
2.0.0
1.5.0
2.1.0
1.0.0
3.0.0
2.1.0
1.2.0

Verification state (after agent finishes):
• /home/user/releases/latest_versions.txt exists with permissions 644 and the following exact byte sequence (including the trailing newline):

3.0.0\n
2.1.0\n
2.0.0\n
1.5.0\n
1.2.0\n
1.0.0\n
